educationalNotes.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
educationalNotes.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent fully connected layers.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the model.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 64 kernels of size 3x3 convolving over 3 channels.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: One learnable bias parameter per filter.

calculationSteps.label: Total Parameters
calculationSteps.explanation: Sum of weights and biases.

educationalNotes.detailed: Slides 64 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 64 kernels of size 3x3 convolving over 64 channels.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: One learnable bias parameter per filter.

calculationSteps.label: Total Parameters
calculationSteps.explanation: Sum of weights and biases.

educationalNotes.detailed: Slides 64 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

educationalNotes.summary: Halves spatial resolution while retaining peak features.
educationalNotes.detailed: Slides a 2x2 window with stride 2 over each feature map, outputting the maximum value in that cell. Downsamples resolution, decreasing compute while preserving dominant signals.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Pooling achieves downsampling and feature invariance with zero learnable parameters.

educationalNotes.summary: Halves spatial resolution while retaining peak features.
educationalNotes.detailed: Slides a 2x2 window with stride 2 over each feature map, outputting the maximum value in that cell. Downsamples resolution, decreasing compute while preserving dominant signals.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Pooling achieves downsampling and feature invariance with zero learnable parameters.

educationalNotes.summary: Unrolls spatial features into a 1D vector.
educationalNotes.detailed: Converts the 3D tensor of shape 7x7x512 into a single vector of length 25088. This step transitions the model from spatial feature mapping to multi-class classification reasoning.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Bridges local convolutions and dense classifiers.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Flattening discards spatial dimensions while preserving the raw activation vector.

calculationSteps.label: Fully Connected Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Every input element is connected to all 4096 output units via a learnable weight.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: One bias term per output unit.

calculationSteps.label: Total Parameters
calculationSteps.explanation: Combined weights and biases.

educationalNotes.detailed: This single layer accounts for over 70% of VGG's total parameter count. It performs global visual reasoning across the entire set of flattened features, determining how parts relate to classes.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: The massive parameter footprint of this transition is why modern models prefer Global Average Pooling over Flattening.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Fully connected layers contain the majority of standard CNN parameters.

calculationSteps.label: Fully Connected Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Every unit from FC1 is connected to all 4096 units of FC2.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: One bias term per output unit.

calculationSteps.label: Total Parameters
calculationSteps.explanation: Combined weights and biases.

educationalNotes.summary: Second dense layer representing high-level semantic abstractions.
educationalNotes.detailed: Performs further non-linear mapping on the 4096-dimensional global visual representations, building high-level classification templates.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Deep fully connected layers learn abstract visual classification features.

calculationSteps.label: Fully Connected Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Every unit from FC2 is connected to the 1000 final output class units.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: One bias term per classification class.

calculationSteps.label: Total Parameters
calculationSteps.explanation: Combined weights and biases.

educationalNotes.detailed: The final network stage. Converts learned features into a probability distribution over 1000 ImageNet categories.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Provides interpretable confidence scores representing the model's decisions.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Softmax maps outputs to a valid probability distribution that sums to 1.
